ab: ya makeyneynaaapan si maran do ili namen.
ch: 我的叔叔是部落的獵人。
en: My dad is a hunter of the tribe.

ab: no kalialikey ko pa am, no makoyab am mayai do vahay namen si maran a somivah si ama, apen da o paltog a ka no zingki a miyowyaw.
ch: 我小的時候，叔叔常會在晚上來家裡約爸爸，他們帶著魚槍和手電筒出門。
en: When I was young, my uncle used to come home at night to ask Dad out. They went out with a fish gun and a flashlight

ab: no avak no ahep am, ioli da o niaaapa da do vahay namen.
ch: 三更半夜時，他們就會帶著獵到的魚獵回來。
en: At daybreak, they returned with their prey.

ab: nomakatakaw am vaoyo o nipaltogan da, nomakatakaw am pai nojia koyta o nisowan da, no to da sapa am anos.
ch: 有時候他們射到的是鮪魚、有時候是捉到龍蝦和章魚，偶爾也會有魷魚。
en: Sometimes it was tuna, sometimes lobster and octopus, and occasionally squid.

ab: no maraw am mida ipanci sira zipos namen a mai do vahay namen a maci’angang.
ch: 白天他們會請親戚朋友來家裡，分享給族人一起吃。
en: During the day, they would invite relatives and friends to come to their homes and share the prey with their people.

ab: no macita ko si maran a mai do vahay namen am, pasaozen ko si ama a mapehza jiaken a mangday mipaltog.
ch: 我每次看到叔叔來家裡都會吵著爸爸，帶著我一起去射魚。
en: Every time I saw my uncle coming to my house, I would quarrel with my father and take me with me to go fishing.

ab: no maniring si ama am “ji topanomanoma o ciring ta makaniow.” no makaveyvow ko rana am ori ipaciboang ko.
ch: 爸爸說：「話不可先說出口，這是禁忌。」等我長大了就可以學習。
en: Dad said, \"Don’t say anything first, it’s taboo.\" I can learn when I grow up.

ab: sira memehakay do ili namen am, maloalovot sira mitawaz do awa ipiyakan so vayo a among no asa kaili.
ch: 部落的男人也會一起出海網魚，讓整個部落都有魚吃。
en: The men of the tribe also go out to sea to net fish, so that the whole tribe has fish to eat.

ab: ya mateneng si ama a mamaring so paltog do awa, si maran a wari ni ama am, ya manineng a mipaltog do kalilian, ori da kasanib a meyeyheza mangahahap.
ch: 我的爸爸很會製作漁槍，我的叔叔也就是爸爸的弟弟射魚很準，所以他們才會常結伴去捕魚。
en: My dad is very good at making Harpoon. My uncle, my father’s younger brother, is fishing very accurate, so they often go fishing together.

ab: to ko akzeba ka pakaveyvow ko rana a maciangay jira mipaltog, ori ikasanib ko a omtahataha a tomezgek do kalilian, ji nimeyyahahay a kapakanet na rana no ineynawa ko a somwat so kono do kalilian.
ch: 為了可以趕快和大人一起去射魚，我常常練習游泳潛水，很快地我的肺活量已可在海裡礁岩挖硨磲貝了。
en: In order to go hunting with adults as soon as possible, I often practiced swimming and diving, and soon my lung capacity was ready to dig for shellfish in the rocks of the sea.

ab: macita na ni ama am payaiten na yaken, no mavyay o tao am ipamaring ko imo so paltog mo an.
ch: 爸爸看到後鼓勵我，等我以後長大了一定會幫我做魚槍。
en: My father encouraged me when he saw it. Told me when I grow up, he would make me a shotgun.

ab: ya ko rana makaveyvow am, ya mian rana so paltog ko a maciheza jira ama a ka ni maran a mangay mipaltog.
ch: 現在我長大了，我有魚槍了，也有機會跟著爸爸和叔叔一起去射魚。
en: Now that I’m older, I’ve got a fish gun and a chance to go fishing with my father and uncle.

ab: no mang-id namen rana a nimakamong am, magaga si ina a ka nira akay.
ch: 我們獵魚上岸後回到家裡，媽媽和爺爺他們都很高興。
en: When we got home from fishing, Mom and Grandpa were very happy.

ab: sira akay a ka ni akes am ipanlanlag da yaken, isaray do tao do to a niomzat jiamen do kalilian a manta ni yamen so sinaaapan.
ch: 爺爺奶奶也會給我祝福，並且感謝天神保佑我們海裡射魚，賜給我們有很多的魚獵。
en: My grandparents will also bless me, and thank the gods for blessing us to shoot fish in the sea, give us a lot of prey.
